temporal_description:
(00:00-00:04) A young woman with voluminous blonde hair and a dark cardigan sits at a table in a busy outdoor cafe. She looks down at the table, speaking to a man whose back is to the camera. She tells him she's glad she never dated him, implying she would have been a disposable item. (Storyline: The scene opens on a tense conversation between a man and a woman. The woman is expressing her regret and frustration about a past relationship, setting a confrontational tone.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: Resigned, slightly bitter. 2) Background sounds or music: The indistinct chatter and noise of a busy public space.)
(00:05-00:09) The woman continues speaking, her expression a mix of sadness and finality. She explains that she would have been a woman he had to get rid of, emphasizing her feeling of being disposable. (Storyline: The woman's dialogue reveals the depth of her past relationship and her feelings of being used, which is the central conflict of the scene.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: Matter-of-fact, with a hint of bitterness. 2) Background sounds or music: Continued ambient noise of the cafe.)
